topic: Two colleagues are both considered for the same promotion
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with an unnerving intensity, casting sharp shadows across the polished floor. Sarah clutched the manila folder so tightly her knuckles were white. Every time Mark walked past her desk, she felt a sudden, sharp intake of breath, a tightening in her chest that made it difficult to draw a full breath. She replayed the meeting notes in her head, dissecting every word, searching for a flaw, a missed opportunity. The silence in the breakroom felt vast, amplifying the frantic drumming in her own ears. When her name was called, the sound echoed too loudly, and her legs felt unsteady beneath her. She offered a practiced, bright smile, but inside, a cold knot was solidifying, pressing against her sternum.

[story 2]

Liam stared at the framed commendation on the wall, the silver gleaming mockingly under the harsh overhead lighting. He kept running a hand through his already disheveled hair, the movement jerky and uncontrolled. The air in the conference room seemed thin, suffocating. He watched Clara speak, her confidence radiating outward like heat, and a strange, hollow feeling settled in his stomach. When the decision was finally announced, the words seemed to arrive in slow motion. He managed a nod, his throat suddenly dry, and he focused intensely on the pattern of the carpet, willing himself to remain still, to become invisible.

[story 3]

Maya fiddled incessantly with the edge of her keyboard, the plastic cool against her fingertips. The entire hallway felt like it was closing in; the footsteps of her peers seemed amplified, each one a judgment. She kept glancing toward the door, anticipating the moment the manager would emerge, the moment the verdict would be delivered. A tremor ran through her hands, causing her to briefly drop her pen. When the name of the chosen candidate was read aloud, Maya felt a profound stillness descend, a sudden inability to process the sound. She simply lowered her gaze, her posture shrinking slightly as if trying to occupy less space in the room.
